reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully encodes the informal statement. R being a UFD is captured by `CommRing R`, `IsDomain R`, and `UniqueFactorizationMonoid R`, which is the standard Mathlib encoding. `FractionRing R` is the field of fractions of the integral domain R (i.e., the "quotient field"). "Isomorphic to ℝ" is naturally expressed as `Nonempty (_ ≃+* ℝ)`, which is the standard way to express "there exists a ring isomorphism"; for fields, ring-isomorphism coincides with field-isomorphism. The hypothesis `Nonempty ((FractionRing R) ≃+* ℝ)` and conclusion `Nonempty (R ≃+* ℝ)` match the informal hypothesis and conclusion respectively. No hidden hypotheses added or dropped.
